configEditor.intro: Read your printer's live configuration straight from Moonraker — every file, parsed into sections and parameters, with structural problems flagged. Read-only for now; guided editing arrives next.

steps: Pick a config file from the dropdown — printer.cfg is selected by default.
Browse its sections; click one to expand its parameters.
Check the Validation banner for structural issues like a duplicate section.
Switch to Raw to read the exact file text.

sections.title: Sections & parameters
sections.body: A Klipper config is a list of [section] blocks, each holding key: value parameters. This widget parses every section — including ones with a name like [tmc2209 stepper_x] — and lists its parameters with their value and any inline comment. Multi-line values (like a macro's gcode:) are kept intact.

includes.title: Included files
includes.body: printer.cfg often pulls in other files with [include other.cfg] (or a glob like [include configs/*.cfg]). Each include is shown as its own entry; pick the included file from the dropdown to inspect what it defines.

validation.title: Validation
validation.body: After parsing, the config is checked for structural problems that are wrong regardless of the section type — a duplicate section header, an empty section name, or a parameter sitting outside any section. These appear in the Validation banner; they don't change your file.

saveConfig.title: The SAVE_CONFIG block
saveConfig.body: Klipper appends an auto-generated block at the end of printer.cfg (after the #*# marker) when you run SAVE_CONFIG — calibration results like PID and Z offsets. It's shown but flagged as auto-saved, and is excluded from the editable section count because you shouldn't hand-edit it.

readonly.title: Read-only (for now)
readonly.body: This first version only reads and displays the config — nothing is written to the printer. Guided, confirm-gated editing (with an automatic backup before any save and a restart afterwards) arrives in a later update.

section.def: A [header] block grouping related settings, e.g. [stepper_x] or [extruder].

parameter.def: A key: value (or key = value) line inside a section, optionally with a trailing # comment.

include.def: A directive that pulls another config file into printer.cfg, so settings can be split across files.

saveConfig.def: The auto-generated block (after #*#) where Klipper stores calibration results — don't hand-edit it.

file.label: Config file
file.emptyList: No config files found on the printer.

edit.hint: Edit the raw text below, then Save. A timestamped backup is made before any write.

save.confirmBody: This overwrites {file} on the printer. A timestamped backup is saved first, and a firmware restart is needed to apply the change.
save.ack: I understand this writes to the printer
save.busy: Refused: the printer is busy (printing or paused). Try again when it's idle.
save.stale: The file changed on disk since it was loaded (a SAVE_CONFIG may have landed, or another app edited it). Saving now would overwrite that change — reload to review (the backup timeline shows what differs).

restart.prompt: Restart the firmware to apply your change?
restart.confirmBody: FIRMWARE_RESTART reloads the config and briefly interrupts the printer. Only do this when it's idle.
restart.busy: Refused: the printer is busy. Restart when it's idle.

states.error: Couldn't read the config from the printer.

catalog.title: Add a config block from the catalog
catalog.hint: Pick a part — its hardware-accurate Klipper block (real run_current, sense_resistor, pin names) is appended to the file for you to review and save.
catalog.inserted: Inserted the block for {name} — review it in Raw and save.

drift.title: What Klipper is running
drift.pending: Klipper has unsaved changes (a SAVE_CONFIG is pending) — saving this file now would clobber them. Run SAVE_CONFIG in the console first.
drift.count: {n} value(s) on disk differ from what's running (edited but not FIRMWARE_RESTARTed):

pinEdit.offBoardHint: This pin isn't in the board's known pin map — check for a typo or an EXP/virtual pin.
pinEdit.doubleHint: This pin is assigned in more than one section.

rule.section_override: Override (defined in multiple files, last wins):

knowledge.stepper: A stepper-motor axis — its driver pins, rotation distance, microsteps, and homing/endstop.
knowledge.tmc: A Trinamic stepper driver — run/hold current, microsteps, and StallGuard/chopper tuning for its stepper.
knowledge.extruder: The hotend and extruder — nozzle size, filament path, heater, thermistor, and PID.
knowledge.heater_bed: The heated bed — its heater pin, thermistor, and PID/temperature limits.
knowledge.fan: The part-cooling fan that cools freshly printed filament.
knowledge.heater_fan: A fan tied to a heater (e.g. hotend cooling) that runs to protect it from overheating.
knowledge.controller_fan: A fan that cools the stepper drivers / electronics while the motors are active.
knowledge.mcu: A microcontroller board running Klipper — its serial or CAN connection.
knowledge.printer: Global machine kinematics and motion limits (type, max velocity, acceleration).
knowledge.bed_mesh: Automatic bed-leveling — probes a grid and compensates for bed flatness while printing.
knowledge.probe: The Z probe used to detect the bed surface for homing and bed mesh.
knowledge.gcode_macro: A custom G-code macro — a named command callable from the console or other macros.
knowledge.output_pin: A controllable output pin (an LED, relay, or enable line, for example).
knowledge.neopixel: Addressable RGB LEDs (NeoPixel / WS2812) and their color control.
knowledge.board_pins: Friendly aliases for a board’s physical pins, referenced elsewhere in the config.
knowledge.input_shaper: Resonance compensation that reduces ringing and ghosting at higher speeds.
knowledge.safe_z_home: Homes Z at a safe XY position (often the bed center) to protect the nozzle and bed.
knowledge.z_tilt: Adjusts multiple Z motors so the gantry/bed plane is level.
knowledge.quad_gantry_level: Levels a four-point gantry so it sits parallel to the bed.
knowledge.filament_switch_sensor: A filament-runout sensor that pauses the print when filament runs out.
knowledge.virtual_sdcard: Lets Klipper print files uploaded to the host (required by Mainsail and Fluidd).

backups.title: Backups ({n})
backups.note: Snapshots are written automatically before each save. Restore loads one into the editor for a final review + save.

sanity.title: Driver value sanity ({n})
sanity.note: Checked {n} TMC driver section(s). A driver with no curated ceiling and a stepper with no assigned motor are skipped, not guessed.

rule.over_driver_cap: {section}: run_current {run_current} A exceeds the driver’s ~{cap} A full-scale ceiling.
rule.near_driver_cap: {section}: run_current {run_current} A is within 10% of the driver’s ~{cap} A ceiling.
rule.over_motor_rating: {section}: run_current {run_current} A exceeds the {motor} rating ({max_current} A).
rule.near_motor_rating: {section}: run_current {run_current} A is within 10% of the {motor} rating ({max_current} A).
rule.odd_microsteps: {section}: microsteps {microsteps} is not a power of two (1…256).

panels.failed: Some panels could not load and are hidden: {panels}. What they would check is unknown right now — not necessarily fine.
